question: Given the week's new notes (8 across ideas/patterns/articles/questions), find 3-5 strong connections. Each connection must be one of Type A (same principle in different domains), Type B (interesting contradiction), Type C (3+ notes into one insight), Type D (question accidentally answered).
context: New notes this week: (1) ideas/CHIEF as Codebase Pattern, (2) patterns/Reflexion Loop in EA Work, (3) articles/Apple WWDC 2026 Siri Rebuilt, (4) ideas/M3 Edge, (5) questions/Does MSA Signal Decay, (6) patterns/Long-Horizon Patterns, (7) numbers/M3 SWE-Bench Pro 59%, (8) ideas/AI Infrastructure as Moat.

connections.bridge: AI Infrastructure as Moat and Apple WWDC 2026 Siri Rebuilt are the same move — when the model layer commoditizes, the durable value migrates up the stack (compute infrastructure for SoftBank, UX layer for Apple).
M3 Edge claims the capability shift is real; Does MSA Signal Decay asks whether that capability survives at 1M context. Both are right; the tension is the operational constraint.

Long-Horizon Patterns, Reflexion Loop, and M3 SWE-Bench Pro 59% all point to the same insight: the binding constraint is no longer raw capability, it is the discipline of staying engaged across hours of work without bailing.

Does MSA Signal Decay asks whether long-context attention survives — and CHIEF as Codebase Pattern answers it from a different angle: if the structure is in the notes, the model doesn't have to hold it all in attention.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) patterns/Compounding via Capture, (2) ideas/Tldraw as Vault Bridge, (3) articles/Sysdig LLM Attack, (4) questions/When Does Structure Become the Work, (5) ideas/Mavis Apex Architecture, (6) patterns/Native > Wrapper.

connections.bridge: Native > Wrapper (design principle) and Sysdig LLM Attack (capability proof) are the same insight on both sides — native access to the substrate (vision, GUI, shell) wins over wrapped abstractions, for both offense and defense.

Compounding via Capture, When Does Structure Become the Work, and Tldraw as Vault Bridge together describe the same discipline: every artifact is a chance to add structure, and the cost of not adding it is invisible until the system breaks.
sources: questions/When Does Structure Become the Work

connections.bridge: Mavis Apex Architecture wants more structure (custom MCPs, MOCs, state-of-mavis); When Does Structure Become the Work asks if there is a point where the structure is the burden. The tension is real and unresolved.
sources: questions/When Does Structure Become the Work

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/Step 2 Autonomy, (2) patterns/Action Without Fresh Perception, (3) questions/What Metrics Define EA Working, (4) numbers/950 FDA AI Devices, (5) articles/SoftBank France 75B, (6) patterns/Tool Surface = Audit Surface.

connections.bridge: Step 2 Autonomy wants Mavis to take more action without asking; Action Without Fresh Perception is the disease that says action without fresh context is dangerous. The tension is the Step 2 conversation's core.

950 FDA AI Devices (75% radiology) and SoftBank France 75B (data center build) both say: the AI revolution is being deployed in the boring industries (medical imaging, infrastructure), not the flashy consumer ones. The companies that win are the ones building the picks-and-shovels.

Tool Surface = Audit Surface and What Metrics Define EA Working are the same principle at different layers — what you expose (tool surface) and what you measure (success metric) are the same audit question, and getting either wrong corrupts the system.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) patterns/Reflexion Loop, (2) ideas/Paged Memory Pattern, (3) articles/M3 Launch Blog, (4) numbers/12h Paper Reproduction, (5) questions/Does M3 Hold 1M Context, (6) patterns/Mixture of Agents.

connections.bridge: M3 Launch Blog, 12h Paper Reproduction, and Does M3 Hold 1M Context together characterize M3's bet: 1M context + native multimodality + long-horizon, and the question is whether the context actually delivers or whether the architecture degrades in the middle.
Reflexion Loop and Paged Memory Pattern are two ways to handle the same problem: how to retain what matters across bounded attention. Reflexion is per-task, paged memory is per-context.
Does M3 Hold 1M Context asks whether the context actually holds — and Mixture of Agents answers it from a different angle: if you can't trust one 1M context, can you trust five 200K contexts?

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/Self-Evolving Skills, (2) patterns/SkillOpt Loop, (3) articles/Microsoft SkillOpt Paper, (4) questions/Which Skills to Train First, (5) numbers/SkillOpt 23.5 Point Gain, (6) ideas/Process Over Outcome.

connections.bridge: Self-Evolving Skills, SkillOpt Loop, and Microsoft SkillOpt Paper together describe a new layer of agent design: optimize the skill text, not the model weights. This is the discipline that makes every other workflow compound.

Process Over Outcome says the value is in the discipline; Which Skills to Train First says the value is in the choice. SkillOpt 23.5 Point Gain says the value is in the measurement. All three are right; the meta-rule is discipline of choice, measured.
Reflexion Loop and SkillOpt Loop are the same feedback loop at different layers — Reflexion extracts lessons per task, SkillOpt extracts rules per skill.

question: Find 3-5 connections in the week's notes.
context: New notes: (1) ideas/CHIEF Pattern Generalizes, (2) patterns/Native > Wrapper, (3) articles/CuaDriver 0.2.0, (4) questions/Why is cu Toggle Off, (5) numbers/M3 OSWorld 70.06%, (6) ideas/Multimodal GUI Loop.

connections.bridge: Multimodal GUI Loop and Native > Wrapper are the same thesis — design-around-the-capability (M3 vision, M3 GUI), not design-around-the-limits. CuaDriver 0.2.0 is the tool, M3 OSWorld 70.06% is the benchmark proof.

CHIEF Pattern Generalizes (structure-in-the-notes) and Multimodal GUI Loop (structure-in-the-action) are two layers of the same principle: add structure at the moment of capture, never retrofit. The question is whether the operator can hold both layers in their head.

Why is cu Toggle Off asks why the capability isn't usable — and CuaDriver 0.2.0 is the answer waiting in the queue (installed, disabled pending macOS accessibility grant). The answer is structural, not technical.
